vulnerability.title: Add noexec Option to /home
vulnerability.description: The noexec mount option can be used to prevent binaries from being executed out of /home. Add the noexec option to the fourth column of /etc/fstab for the line which controls mounting of /home.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if ( ! ( { rpm --quiet -q kernel-core ;} && { rpm --quiet -q rpm-ostree ;} && { rpm --quiet -q bootc ;} && { ! rpm --quiet -q openshift-kubelet ;} && ([ -f /run/ostree-booted ] || [ -L /ostree ]) ) && ! ( [ -f /.dockerenv ] || [ -f /run/.containerenv ] ) ); then function perform_remediation { # the mount point /home has to be defined in /etc/fstab # before this remediation can be executed. In case it is not defined, the # remediation aborts and no changes regarding the mount point are done. mount_point_match_regexp="$(printf "^[[:space:]]*[^#].*[[:space:]]%s[[:space:]]" "/home")" grep "$mount_point_match_regexp" -q /etc/fstab \ || { echo "The mount point '/home' is not even in /etc/fstab, so we can't set up mount options" >&2; echo "Not

triage_decision.reason: Adding the noexec mount option to /home is a standard security hardening practice that prevents execution of binaries from user home directories, reducing malware risks. It is unlikely to cause system lockout or break critical services, as /home is typically for user data, not system executables.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; Potential disruption to user applications that rely on executing scripts or binaries from /home; Requires system reboot or remount to apply changes

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
The finding is about adding the 'noexec' option to the /home mount in /etc/fstab. I need to:

1. Read /etc/fstab to see the current /home mount line
2. Modify it to add 'noexec' to the mount options
3. Possibly need to remount /home or reboot for changes to take effect

Let me start by reading /etc/fstab to see the current configuration.
